name: Manoa
desc: Lush rainforest valley with UH campus vibes — cooler temps, gorgeous hiking, charming older homes, feels worlds away from Waikiki.
insider: Manoa Falls trail is packed by 10am — go at 7am or try the less-known Aihualama connector trail to Pauoa Flats
Andy's Sandwiches near UH is an underrated institution since 1977 — get the avocado or tuna melt
Rain here is almost daily but usually brief afternoon showers — mold and mildew are real concerns for renters
Lyon Arboretum is free with reservation and almost nobody goes — the native Hawaiian plant collection is extraordinary
Waioli Tea Room serves a charming old-school lunch in a historic cottage — the atmosphere is pure old Hawaii
The UH Manoa campus Japanese garden near the East-West Center is one of the most peaceful spots in Honolulu
Manoa Valley Theatre is a beloved community theater in an old church — productions are surprisingly excellent
Expect 10-15 degrees cooler than Waikiki and up to 150 inches of rain per year — this valley is genuinely wet
Morning rainbows arching over the valley are a near-daily occurrence — Manoa literally means 'vast' in Hawaiian
Parking near UH is a nightmare during the semester — park on Oahu Ave or East Manoa Road and walk in
Obama grew up nearby and spent time in Manoa — the neighborhood is full of quiet local history
